recent_messages.content:
You are level-2 BUILDER. STRICT: NO sub-of-sub delegation. Do NOT call Task/subagents. Work directly.

Task:
Implement W3-I1 Swarm surface in `src/dashboard-v2` as the first implementation lane under W3 kickoff.

Scope:
- You may edit only files under `src/dashboard-v2/src/`.
- Prefer existing architecture patterns already used by `panels/*`, `components/MainPanel.tsx`, `constants.ts`, `i18n.ts`, `snapshot.ts`, `types.ts`, `hooks/useDashboardData.ts`, `index.tsx`.
- You may reference legacy behavior from:
  - `src/dashboard/components/SwarmMonitor.tsx`
  - `src/dashboard/views/SwarmOrchestratorView.tsx`
- Do NOT edit docs in this task.
- Do NOT edit files outside `src/dashboard-v2/src/`.

Execution strategy (sequential, dependency-aware):
1) Add/implement Swarm surface panel(s) for dashboard-v2 (status + orchestration visibility) with deterministic fallback states.
2) Wire tab/view registration so user can navigate to Swarm surface from existing dashboard-v2 UI.
3) Ensure keyboard/tab navigation and render flow do not regress existing panels.
4) Keep implementation compact and aligned with current panel style.

Acceptance criteria:
A1) Swarm surface is reachable in dashboard-v2 through the existing tab/panel flow.
A2) Panel renders meaningful swarm/orchestration signals from available snapshot/state data, with explicit empty/fallback states.
A3) No regression to existing panels and navigation contracts.
A4) No file changes outside `src/dashboard-v2/src/`.
A5) Verification command passes: `cd src/dashboard-v2 && bunx tsc --noEmit`.

Evidence required:
- `git status --short` scoped to changed files.
- `git diff --name-only` and focused diff summary.
- Verification output for `bunx tsc --noEmit`.

Return format (exact):
- Outcome: success|partial|failure
- Files changed
- Implementation summary (4-7 bullets)
- Acceptance check A1..A5 pass/fail
- Evidence (commands + key output)
- Risks/notes (max 3)

Constraint footer:
ABSOLUTE BAN: delegated sub-sessions may NOT delegate further. No sub-of-sub delegation allowed. All delegated outputs must return directly to main orchestrator session.
